items.title: Gary Trent Jr. had one of his worst seasons last year (single-digit PPG for the first time since 2019-20 + his MPG have dipped over 4 straight seasons). DARKO career arc is something. Very curious he gets $64 million guaranteed in an offseason when guaranteed money is hard to get

DARKO career arc is something. Very curious he gets $64 million guaranteed in an offseason when guaranteed money is hard to get.

items.title: The Bucks received luxury tax relief in the form of a set-off from the dead cap hit of Damian Lillard.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Bucks received luxury tax relief in the form of a set-off from the dead cap hit of Damian Lillard.

$6 million is deducted evenly over the five-year stretched term, including last season's.

His $22.5 million annual dead cap hit through 2029-30 is reduced to $21.3 million.

items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
Gary Trent Jr. becomes one of the few players to break out of the "minimum zone" with a big deal following two seasons on the veteran minimum. 

He is now the 4th-highest paid player on the Bucks behind Tyler Herro ($33 million), Myles Turner ($26.4 million) & Kyle Kuzma ($20.4 million).

items.title: Milwaukee Bucks salary cap after re-signing Gary Trent Jr. & Pete Nance.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Milwaukee Bucks salary cap after re-signing Gary Trent Jr. & Pete Nance.

They use up a good chunk of their tax flexibility and project to be just $11 million under the luxury tax if Trent gets standard raises.

They've also got a roster crunch with 16 guaranteed contracts.

items.title: The Milwaukee Bucks and Gary Trent Jr. have agreed to a four-year, $64 million deal, league sources confirm. The deal is fully guaranteed with no options, I’m told. Klutch Sports Group CEO Rich Paul and agent Lucas Newton worked with Bucks officials on the deal. ESPN first.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Milwaukee Bucks and Gary Trent Jr. have agreed to a four-year, $64 million deal, league sources confirm. The deal is fully guaranteed with no options, I’m told. Klutch Sports Group CEO Rich Paul and agent Lucas Newton worked with Bucks officials on the deal. ESPN first.

items.title: The Bucks and Trent's reps worked through negotiations since the NBA Finals ended in mid-June while also sorting through sign-and-trade possibilities from interested teams over the last week. The sides moved toward completing a new deal together Saturday.

The Magic return to Cox Pavilion tomorrow for a back-to-back against Portland.

Lester Quinones leads Orlando with 11 points while both Jase Richardson and Colin Castleton have 8 apiece.

items.title: Ja Morant about what he learned from his new teammates: "They ragebait a lot. They ragebait a lot, for sure."
items.author: MrBuckBuck
items.body_excerpt: Full quote: "They ragebait a lot. They ragebait a lot, for sure. I feel it's just to turn a competitive, you know, edge, to us. Deni (Avdija) definitely number one, but he for sure like ragebait people to try to make you respond, or get you mad, so when we have to play or…

items.title: A day after a thrilling OT win to start Summer League, the Celtics just wrapped up today’s practice.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
A day after a thrilling OT win to start Summer League, the Celtics just wrapped up today’s practice.

We’ll speak with a couple of players about it shortly.
